NKJV.1: You therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
NKJV.2: And the things that you have heard from me among many witnesses, commit these to faithful men who will be able to teach others also.
NKJV.3: You therefore must endure hardship as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.
NKJV.4: No one engaged in warfare entangles himself with the affairs of this life, that he may please him who enlisted him as a soldier.
NKJV.5: And also if anyone competes in athletics, he is not crowned unless he competes according to the rules.
NKJV.6: The hardworking farmer must be first to partake of the crops.
NKJV.7: Consider what I say, and may the Lord give you understanding in all things.
NKJV.8: Remember that Jesus Christ, of the seed of David, was raised from the dead according to my gospel,
NKJV.9: for which I suffer trouble as an evildoer, even to the point of chains; but the word of God is not chained.
NKJV.10: Therefore I endure all things for the sake of the elect, that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
NKJV.11: This is a faithful saying: For if we died with Him, We shall also live with Him.
NKJV.12: If we endure, We shall also reign with Him. If we deny Him, He also will deny us.
NKJV.13: If we are faithless, He remains faithful; He cannot deny Himself.
NKJV.14: Remind them of these things, charging them before the Lord not to strive about words to no profit, to the ruin of the hearers.
NKJV.15: Be diligent to present yourself approved to God, a worker who does not need to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.
NKJV.16: But shun profane and idle babblings, for they will increase to more ungodliness.
NKJV.17: And their message will spread like cancer. Hymenaeus and Philetus are of this sort,
NKJV.18: who have strayed concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection is already past; and they overthrow the faith of some.
NKJV.19: Nevertheless the solid foundation of God stands, having this seal: “The Lord knows those who are His,” and, “Let everyone who names the name of Christ depart from iniquity.”
NKJV.20: But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay, some for honor and some for dishonor.
NKJV.21: Therefore if anyone cleanses himself from the latter, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified and useful for the Master, prepared for every good work.
NKJV.22: Flee also youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness, faith, love, peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
NKJV.23: But avoid foolish and ignorant disputes, knowing that they generate strife.
NKJV.24: And a servant of the Lord must not quarrel but be gentle to all, able to teach, patient,
NKJV.25: in humility correcting those who are in opposition, if God perhaps will grant them repentance, so that they may know the truth,
NKJV.26: and that they may come to their senses and escape the snare of the devil, having been taken captive by him to do his will.

ESV.1: You then, my child, be strengthened by the grace that is in Christ Jesus,
ESV.2: and what you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses entrust to faithful men,  who will be able to teach others also.
ESV.3: Share in suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
ESV.4: No soldier gets entangled in civilian pursuits, since his aim is to please the one who enlisted him.
ESV.5: An athlete is not crowned unless he competes according to the rules.
ESV.6: It is the hard-working farmer who ought to have the first share of the crops.
ESV.7: Think over what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in everything.
ESV.8: Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, the offspring of David, as preached in my gospel,
ESV.9: for which I am suffering, bound with chains as a criminal. But the word of God is not bound!
ESV.10: Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the elect, that they also may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
ESV.11: The saying is trustworthy, for: If we have died with him, we will also live with him;
ESV.12: if we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny him, he also will deny us;
ESV.13: if we are faithless, he remains faithful —  for he cannot deny himself.
ESV.14: Remind them of these things, and charge them before God  not to quarrel about words, which does no good, but only ruins the hearers.
ESV.15: Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved,  a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly handling the word of truth.
ESV.16: But avoid irreverent babble, for it will lead people into more and more ungodliness,
ESV.17: and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
ESV.18: who have swerved from the truth, saying that the resurrection has already happened. They are upsetting the faith of some.
ESV.19: But God's firm foundation stands, bearing this seal: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let everyone who names the name of the Lord depart from iniquity.”
ESV.20: Now in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver but also of wood and clay, some for honorable use, some for dishonorable.
ESV.21: Therefore, if anyone cleanses himself from what is dishonorable,  he will be a vessel for honorable use, set apart as holy, useful to the master of the house, ready for every good work.
ESV.22: So flee youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
ESV.23: Have nothing to do with foolish, ignorant controversies; you know that they breed quarrels.
ESV.24: And the Lord's servant  must not be quarrelsome but kind to everyone, able to teach, patiently enduring evil,
ESV.25: correcting his opponents with gentleness. God may perhaps grant them repentance leading to a knowledge of the truth,
ESV.26: and they may come to their senses and escape from the snare of the devil, after being captured by him to do his will.

NASB.1: You therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
NASB.2: The things which you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses, entrust these to faithful men who will be able to teach others also.
NASB.3: Suffer hardship with [me], as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
NASB.4: No soldier in active service entangles himself in the affairs of everyday life, so that he may please the one who enlisted him as a soldier.
NASB.5: Also if anyone competes as an athlete, he does not win the prize unless he competes according to the rules.
NASB.6: The hard-working farmer ought to be the first to receive his share of the crops.
NASB.7: Consider what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in everything.
NASB.8: Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, descendant of David, according to my gospel,
NASB.9: for which I suffer hardship even to imprisonment as a criminal; but the word of God is not imprisoned.
NASB.10: For this reason I endure all things for the sake of those who are chosen, so that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus [and] with [it] eternal glory.
NASB.11: It is a trustworthy statement: For if we died with Him, we will also live with Him;
NASB.12: If we endure, we will also reign with Him; If we deny Him, He also will deny us;
NASB.13: If we are faithless, He remains faithful, for He cannot deny Himself.
NASB.14: Remind [them] of these things, and solemnly charge [them] in the presence of God not to wrangle about words, which is useless [and leads] to the ruin of the hearers.
NASB.15: Be diligent to present yourself approved to God as a workman who does not need to be ashamed, accurately handling the word of truth.
NASB.16: But avoid worldly [and] empty chatter, for it will lead to further ungodliness,
NASB.17: and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
NASB.18: [men] who have gone astray from the truth saying that the resurrection has already taken place, and they upset the faith of some.
NASB.19: Nevertheless, the firm foundation of God stands, having this seal, 'The Lord knows those who are His,' and, 'Everyone who names the name of the Lord is to abstain from wickedness.'
NASB.20: Now in a large house there are not only gold and silver vessels, but also vessels of wood and of earthenware, and some to honor and some to dishonor.
NASB.21: Therefore, if anyone cleanses himself from these [things], he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified, useful to the Master, prepared for every good work.
NASB.22: Now flee from youthful lusts and pursue righteousness, faith, love [and] peace, with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
NASB.23: But refuse foolish and ignorant speculations, knowing that they produce quarrels.
NASB.24: The Lord's bond-servant must not be quarrelsome, but be kind to all, able to teach, patient when wronged,
NASB.25: with gentleness correcting those who are in opposition, if perhaps God may grant them repentance leading to the knowledge of the truth,
NASB.26: and they may come to their senses [and escape] from the snare of the devil, having been held captive by him to do his will.

NLT.1: Timothy, my dear son, be strong through the grace that God gives you in Christ Jesus.
NLT.2: You have heard me teach things that have been confirmed by many reliable witnesses. Now teach these truths to other trustworthy people who will be able to pass them on to others.
NLT.3: Endure suffering along with me, as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
NLT.4: Soldiers don’t get tied up in the affairs of civilian life, for then they cannot please the officer who enlisted them.
NLT.5: And athletes cannot win the prize unless they follow the rules.
NLT.6: And hardworking farmers should be the first to enjoy the fruit of their labor.
NLT.7: Think about what I am saying. The Lord will help you understand all these things.
NLT.8: Always remember that Jesus Christ, a descendant of King David, was raised from the dead. This is the Good News I preach.
NLT.9: And because I preach this Good News, I am suffering and have been chained like a criminal. But the word of God cannot be chained.
NLT.10: So I am willing to endure anything if it will bring salvation and eternal glory in Christ Jesus to those God has chosen.
NLT.11:
This is a trustworthy saying: If we die with him,
we will also live with him.
NLT.12:
If we endure hardship,
we will reign with him.
If we deny him,
he will deny us.
NLT.13:
If we are unfaithful,
he remains faithful,
for he cannot deny who he is.
NLT.14: Remind everyone about these things, and command them in God’s presence to stop fighting over words. Such arguments are useless, and they can ruin those who hear them.
NLT.15: Work hard so you can present yourself to God and receive his approval. Be a good worker, one who does not need to be ashamed and who correctly explains the word of truth.
NLT.16: Avoid worthless, foolish talk that only leads to more godless behavior.
NLT.17: This kind of talk spreads like cancer, as in the case of Hymenaeus and Philetus.
NLT.18: They have left the path of truth, claiming that the resurrection of the dead has already occurred; in this way, they have turned some people away from the faith.
NLT.19: But God’s truth stands firm like a foundation stone with this inscription: “The LORD knows those who are his,” and “All who belong to the LORD must turn away from evil.”
NLT.20: In a wealthy home some utensils are made of gold and silver, and some are made of wood and clay. The expensive utensils are used for special occasions, and the cheap ones are for everyday use.
NLT.21: If you keep yourself pure, you will be a special utensil for honorable use. Your life will be clean, and you will be ready for the Master to use you for every good work.
NLT.22: Run from anything that stimulates youthful lusts. Instead, pursue righteous living, faithfulness, love, and peace. Enjoy the companionship of those who call on the Lord with pure hearts.
NLT.23: Again I say, don’t get involved in foolish, ignorant arguments that only start fights.
NLT.24: A servant of the Lord must not quarrel but must be kind to everyone, be able to teach, and be patient with difficult people.
NLT.25: Gently instruct those who oppose the truth. Perhaps God will change those people’s hearts, and they will learn the truth.
NLT.26: Then they will come to their senses and escape from the devil’s trap. For they have been held captive by him to do whatever he wants.

WEB.1: You therefore, my child, be strengthened in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
WEB.2: The things which you have heard from me among many witnesses, commit the same to faithful men, who will be able to teach others also.
WEB.3: You therefore must endure hardship, as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
WEB.4: No soldier on duty entangles himself in the affairs of life, that he may please him who enrolled him as a soldier.
WEB.5: Also, if anyone competes in athletics, he isn’t crowned unless he has competed by the rules.
WEB.6: The farmer who labors must be the first to get a share of the crops.
WEB.7: Consider what I say, and may the Lord give you understanding in all things.
WEB.8: Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, of the offspring  of David, according to my Good News,
WEB.9: in which I suffer hardship to the point of chains as a criminal. But God’s word isn’t chained.
WEB.10: Therefore I endure all things for the chosen ones’ sake, that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
WEB.11: This saying is trustworthy: “For if we died with him, we will also live with him.
WEB.12: If we endure, we will also reign with him. If we deny him, he also will deny us.
WEB.13: If we are faithless, he remains faithful. For he can’t deny himself.”
WEB.14: Remind them of these things, charging them in the sight of the Lord, that they don’t argue about words, to no profit, to the subverting of those who hear.
WEB.15: Give diligence to present yourself approved by God, a workman who doesn’t need to be ashamed, properly handling the Word of Truth.
WEB.16: But shun empty chatter, for it will go further in ungodliness,
WEB.17: and those words will consume like gangrene, of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus;
WEB.18: men who have erred concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection is already past, and overthrowing the faith of some.
WEB.19: However God’s firm foundation stands, having this seal, “The Lord knows those who are his,”  and, “Let every one who names the name of the Lord  depart from unrighteousness.”
WEB.20: Now in a large house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of clay. Some are for honor, and some for dishonor.
WEB.21: If anyone therefore purges himself from these, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified, and suitable for the master’s use, prepared for every good work.
WEB.22: Flee from youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
WEB.23: But refuse foolish and ignorant questionings, knowing that they generate strife.
WEB.24: The Lord’s servant must not quarrel, but be gentle towards all, able to teach, patient,
WEB.25: in gentleness correcting those who oppose him: perhaps God may give them repentance leading to a full knowledge of the truth,
WEB.26: and they may recover themselves out of the devil’s snare, having been taken captive by him to his will.

CSB17.1: You, therefore, my son, be strong in the grace <sup>ⓗ</sup> that is in Christ Jesus.
CSB17.2: What you have heard from me <sup>ⓘ</sup> in the presence of many witnesses, commit to faithful <sup>ⓙ</sup> men <sup>[1]</sup> who will be able to teach others also.
CSB17.3: Share in suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.4: No one serving as a soldier gets entangled in the concerns of civilian life; he seeks to please the commanding officer.
CSB17.5: Also, if anyone competes as an athlete, he is not crowned unless he competes according to the rules.
CSB17.6: The hardworking farmer ought to be the first to get a share of the crops. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.7: Consider what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in everything.
CSB17.8: Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead <sup>ⓜ</sup> and descended from David, according to my gospel,
CSB17.9: for which I suffer to the point of being bound like a criminal. But the word of God is not bound.
CSB17.10: This is why I endure <sup>ⓝ</sup> all things for the elect: <sup>ⓐ</sup> so that they also may obtain salvation, which is in Christ Jesus, with eternal glory. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.11:
This saying is trustworthy: ⓒ For if we died with him, ⓓ
we will also live with him;
CSB17.12:
if we endure, we will also reign ⓔ with him;
if we deny him, he will also deny us;
CSB17.13:
if we are faithless, he remains faithful,
for he cannot deny himself.
CSB17.14: Remind them of these things, and charge them before God <sup>[2]</sup> not to fight about words. <sup>ⓕ</sup> This is useless and leads to the ruin of those who listen.
CSB17.15: Be diligent to present yourself to God as one approved, a worker who doesn’t need to be ashamed, correctly teaching the word of truth. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.16: Avoid irreverent and empty speech, since those who engage in it will produce even more godlessness, <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.17: and their teaching will spread like gangrene. Hymenaeus <sup>ⓘ</sup> and Philetus are among them.
CSB17.18: They have departed from the truth, saying that the resurrection has already taken place, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and are ruining the faith of some. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.19: Nevertheless, God’s solid foundation stands firm, bearing this inscription: ⓛ The Lord knows those who are his, [3] ⓜ and let everyone who calls on the name ⓝ of [4] the Lord turn away from wickedness. ⓐ
CSB17.20: Now in a large house there are not only gold and silver vessels, <sup>ⓑ</sup> but also those of wood and clay; some for honorable <sup>[5]</sup> use and some for dishonorable. <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.21: So if anyone purifies himself from anything dishonorable, <sup>[7]</sup> he will be a special <sup>[8]</sup> instrument, set apart, useful to the Master, prepared for every good work. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.22: Flee <sup>ⓔ</sup> from youthful passions, and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, <sup>ⓕ</sup> along with those who call on the Lord <sup>ⓖ</sup> from a pure heart.
CSB17.23: But reject foolish and ignorant disputes, because you know that they breed quarrels.
CSB17.24: The Lord’s <sup>ⓗ</sup> servant must not quarrel, <sup>ⓘ</sup> but must be gentle to everyone, able to teach, <sup>[9]</sup> and patient,
CSB17.25: instructing his opponents with gentleness. <sup>ⓙ</sup> Perhaps God will grant them repentance <sup>ⓚ</sup> leading them to the knowledge of the truth.
CSB17.26: Then they may come to their senses and escape the trap of the devil, <sup>ⓛ</sup> who has taken them captive to do his will. <sup>ⓜ</sup>

AMP.1: So you, my son, be strong [constantly strengthened] and empowered in the grace that is [to be found only] in Christ Jesus.
AMP.2: The things [the doctrine, the precepts, the admonitions, the sum of my ministry] which you have heard me teach in the presence of many witnesses, entrust [as a treasure] to reliable and faithful men who will also be capable and qualified to teach others.
AMP.3: Take with me your share of hardship [passing through the difficulties which you are called to endure], like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
AMP.4: No soldier in active service gets entangled in the [ordinary business] affairs of civilian life; [he avoids them] so that he may please the one who enlisted him to serve.
AMP.5: And if anyone competes as an athlete [in competitive games], he is not crowned [with the wreath of victory] unless he competes according to the rules.
AMP.6: The hard-working farmer [who labors to produce crops] ought to be the first to receive his share of the crops.
AMP.7: Think over the things I am saying [grasp their application], for the Lord will grant you insight and understanding in everything.
AMP.8: Remember Jesus Christ [the ever-living Lord who has] risen from the dead, [as the prophesied King] descended from David [king of Israel], according to my gospel [the good news that I preach],
AMP.9: for that [gospel] I am suffering even to [the point of] wearing chains like a criminal; but the word of God is not chained or imprisoned!
AMP.10: For this reason I [am ready to] patiently endure all things for the sake of those who are the elect (God's chosen ones), so that they too may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus and with it the reward of eternal glory.
AMP.11:
This is a faithful and trustworthy saying:
If we died with Him, we will also live with Him;
AMP.12:
If we endure, we will also reign with Him;
If we deny Him, He will also deny us;
AMP.13: If we are faithless, He remains faithful [true to His word and His righteous character], for He cannot deny Himself.
AMP.14: Remind the people of these facts, and solemnly charge them in the presence of God to avoid petty controversy over words, which does no good, and [upsets and undermines and] ruins [the faith of] those who listen.
AMP.15: Study and do your best to present yourself to God approved, a workman [tested by trial] who has no reason to be ashamed, accurately handling and skillfully teaching the word of truth.
AMP.16: But avoid all irreverent babble and godless chatter [with its profane, empty words], for it will lead to further ungodliness,
AMP.17: and their teaching will spread like gangrene. So it is with Hymenaeus and Philetus,
AMP.18: who have deviated from the truth. They claim that the resurrection has already taken place, and they undermine the faith of some.
AMP.19: Nevertheless, the firm foundation of God [which He has laid] stands [sure and unshaken despite attacks], bearing this seal: "The Lord knows those who are His," and, "Let everyone who names the name of the Lord stand apart from wickedness and withdraw from wrongdoing."
AMP.20: Now in a large house there are not only vessels and objects of gold and silver, but also vessels and objects of wood and of earthenware, and some are for honorable (noble, good) use and some for dishonorable (ignoble, common).
AMP.21: Therefore, if anyone cleanses himself from these things [which are dishonorable—disobedient, sinful], he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified [set apart for a special purpose and], useful to the Master, prepared for every good work.
AMP.22: Run away from youthful lusts—pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those [believers] who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
AMP.23: But have nothing to do with foolish and ignorant speculations [useless disputes over unedifying, stupid controversies], since you know that they produce strife and give birth to quarrels.
AMP.24: The servant of the Lord must not participate in quarrels, but must be kind to everyone [even-tempered, preserving peace, and he must be], skilled in teaching, patient and tolerant when wronged.
AMP.25: He must correct those who are in opposition with courtesy and gentleness in the hope that God may grant that they will repent and be led to the knowledge of the truth [accurately understanding and welcoming it],
AMP.26: and that they may come to their senses and escape from the trap of the devil, having been held captive by him to do his will.

MSG.1: So, my son, throw yourself into this work for Christ.
MSG.2: Pass on what you heard from me—the whole congregation saying Amen!—to reliable leaders who are competent to teach others.
MSG.3: When the going gets rough, take it on the chin with the rest of us, the way Jesus did.
MSG.4: A soldier on duty doesn't get caught up in making deals at the marketplace. He concentrates on carrying out orders.
MSG.5: An athlete who refuses to play by the rules will never get anywhere.
MSG.6: It's the diligent farmer who gets the produce.
MSG.7: Think it over. God will make it all plain.
MSG.8: Fix this picture firmly in your mind: Jesus, descended from the line of David, raised from the dead. It's what you've heard from me all along.
MSG.9: It's what I'm sitting in jail for right now—but God's Word isn't in jail!
MSG.10: That's why I stick it out here—so that everyone God calls will get in on the salvation of Christ in all its glory.
MSG.11: This is a sure thing: If we die with him, we'll live with him;
MSG.12: If we stick it out with him, we'll rule with him; If we turn our backs on him, he'll turn his back on us;
MSG.13: If we give up on him, he does not give up— for there's no way he can be false to himself.
MSG.14: Repeat these basic essentials over and over to God's people. Warn them before God against pious nitpicking, which chips away at the faith. It just wears everyone out.
MSG.15: Concentrate on doing your best for God, work you won't be ashamed of, laying out the truth plain and simple.
MSG.16: Stay clear of pious talk that is only talk. Words are not mere words, you know. If they're not backed by a godly life,
MSG.17: they accumulate as poison in the soul. Hymenaeus and Philetus are examples,
MSG.18: throwing believers off stride and missing the truth by a mile by saying the resurrection is over and done with.
MSG.19: Meanwhile, God's firm foundation is as firm as ever, these sentences engraved on the stones: god knows who belongs to him. spurn evil, all you who name god as god.
MSG.20: In a well-furnished kitchen there are not only crystal goblets and silver platters, but waste cans and compost buckets—some containers used to serve fine meals, others to take out the garbage.
MSG.21: Become the kind of container God can use to present any and every kind of gift to his guests for their blessing.
MSG.22: Run away from infantile indulgence. Run after mature righteousness—faith, love, peace—joining those who are in honest and serious prayer before God.
MSG.23: Refuse to get involved in inane discussions; they always end up in fights.
MSG.24: God's servant must not be argumentative, but a gentle listener and a teacher who keeps cool,
MSG.25: working firmly but patiently with those who refuse to obey. You never know how or when God might sober them up with a change of heart and a turning to the truth,
MSG.26: enabling them to escape the Devil's trap, where they are caught and held captive, forced to run his errands.

NIV.1: You then, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
NIV.2: And the things you have heard me say in the presence of many witnesses entrust to reliable men who will also be qualified to teach others.
NIV.3: Endure hardship with us like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
NIV.4: No one serving as a soldier gets involved in civilian affairs—he wants to please his commanding officer.
NIV.5: Similarly, if anyone competes as an athlete, he does not receive the victor’s crown unless he competes according to the rules.
NIV.6: The hardworking farmer should be the first to receive a share of the crops.
NIV.7: Reflect on what I am saying, for the Lord will give you insight into all this.
NIV.8: Remember Jesus Christ, raised from the dead, descended from David. This is my gospel,
NIV.9: for which I am suffering even to the point of being chained like a criminal. But God’s word is not chained.
NIV.10: Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the elect, that they too may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus, with eternal glory.
NIV.11:
Here is a trustworthy saying:
If we died with him,
we will also live with him;
NIV.12:
if we endure,
we will also reign with him.
If we disown him,
he will also disown us;
NIV.13:
if we are faithless,
he will remain faithful,
for he cannot disown himself.
NIV.14:
A Workman Approved by God
Keep reminding them of these things. Warn them before God against quarreling about words; it is of no value, and only ruins those who listen.
NIV.15: Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved, a workman who does not need to be ashamed and who correctly handles the word of truth.
NIV.16: Avoid godless chatter, because those who indulge in it will become more and more ungodly.
NIV.17: Their teaching will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
NIV.18: who have wandered away from the truth. They say that the resurrection has already taken place, and they destroy the faith of some.
NIV.19: Nevertheless, God’s solid foundation stands firm, sealed with this inscription: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Everyone who confesses the name of the Lord must turn away from wickedness.”
NIV.20: In a large house there are articles not only of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay; some are for noble purposes and some for ignoble.
NIV.21: If a man cleanses himself from the latter, he will be an instrument for noble purposes, made holy, useful to the Master and prepared to do any good work.
NIV.22: Flee the evil desires of youth, and pursue righteousness, faith, love and peace, along with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
NIV.23: Don’t have anything to do with foolish and stupid arguments, because you know they produce quarrels.
NIV.24: And the Lord’s servant must not quarrel; instead, he must be kind to everyone, able to teach, not resentful.
NIV.25: Those who oppose him he must gently instruct, in the hope that God will grant them repentance leading them to a knowledge of the truth,
NIV.26: and that they will come to their senses and escape from the trap of the devil, who has taken them captive to do his will.

NRSVCE.1: You then, my child, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus;
NRSVCE.2: and what you have heard from me through many witnesses entrust to faithful people who will be able to teach others as well.
NRSVCE.3: Share in suffering like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.4: No one serving in the army gets entangled in everyday affairs; the soldier’s aim is to please the enlisting officer.
NRSVCE.5: And in the case of an athlete, no one is crowned without competing according to the rules.
NRSVCE.6: It is the farmer who does the work who ought to have the first share of the crops.
NRSVCE.7: Think over what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.
NRSVCE.8: Remember Jesus Christ, raised from the dead, a descendant of David — that is my gospel,
NRSVCE.9: for which I suffer hardship, even to the point of being chained like a criminal. But the word of God is not chained.
NRSVCE.10: Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the elect, so that they may also obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus, with eternal glory.
NRSVCE.11: The saying is sure: If we have died with him, we will also live with him;
NRSVCE.12:
if we endure, we will also reign with him;
if we deny him, he will also deny us;
NRSVCE.13:
if we are faithless, he remains faithful —
for he cannot deny himself.
NRSVCE.14: Remind them of this, and warn them before God that they are to avoid wrangling over words, which does no good but only ruins those who are listening.
NRSVCE.15: Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved by him, a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly explaining the word of truth.
NRSVCE.16: Avoid profane chatter, for it will lead people into more and more impiety,
NRSVCE.17: and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
NRSVCE.18: who have swerved from the truth by claiming that the resurrection has already taken place. They are upsetting the faith of some.
NRSVCE.19: But God’s firm foundation stands, bearing this inscription: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let everyone who calls on the name of the Lord turn away from wickedness.”
NRSVCE.20: In a large house there are utensils not only of gold and silver but also of wood and clay, some for special use, some for ordinary.
NRSVCE.21: All who cleanse themselves of the things I have mentioned will become special utensils, dedicated and useful to the owner of the house, ready for every good work.
NRSVCE.22: Shun youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
NRSVCE.23: Have nothing to do with stupid and senseless controversies; you know that they breed quarrels.
NRSVCE.24: And the Lord’s servant must not be quarrelsome but kindly to everyone, an apt teacher, patient,
NRSVCE.25: correcting opponents with gentleness. God may perhaps grant that they will repent and come to know the truth,
NRSVCE.26: and that they may escape from the snare of the devil, having been held captive by him to do his will.

NET.1: So you, my child, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
NET.2: And entrust what you heard me say in the presence of many others as witnesses to faithful people who will be competent to teach others as well.
NET.3: Take your share of suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
NET.4: No one in military service gets entangled in matters of everyday life; otherwise he will not please the one who recruited him.
NET.5: Also, if anyone competes as an athlete, he will not be crowned as the winner unless he competes according to the rules.
NET.6: The farmer who works hard ought to have the first share of the crops.
NET.7: Think about what I am saying and the Lord will give you understanding of all this.
NET.8: Remember Jesus Christ, raised from the dead, a descendant of David; such is my gospel,
NET.9: for which I suffer hardship to the point of imprisonment as a criminal, but God’s message is not imprisoned!
NET.10: So I endure all things for the sake of those chosen by God, that they too may obtain salvation in Christ Jesus and its eternal glory.
NET.11: This saying is trustworthy: If we died with him, we will also live with him.
NET.12: If we endure, we will also reign with him. If we deny him, he will also deny us.
NET.13: If we are unfaithful, he remains faithful, since he cannot deny himself.
NET.14: Remind people of these things and solemnly charge them before the Lord not to wrangle over words. This is of no benefit; it just brings ruin on those who listen.
NET.15: Make every effort to present yourself before God as a proven worker who does not need to be ashamed, teaching the message of truth accurately.
NET.16: But avoid profane chatter, because those occupied with it will stray further and further into ungodliness,
NET.17: and their message will spread its infection like gangrene. Hymenaeus and Philetus are in this group.
NET.18: They have strayed from the truth by saying that the resurrection has already occurred, and they are undermining some people’s faith.
NET.19: However, God’s solid foundation remains standing, bearing this seal: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and “Everyone who confesses the name of the Lord must turn away from evil.”
NET.20: Now in a wealthy home there are not only gold and silver vessels, but also ones made of wood and of clay, and some are for honorable use, but others for ignoble use.
NET.21: So if someone cleanses himself of such behavior, he will be a vessel for honorable use, set apart, useful for the Master, prepared for every good work.
NET.22: But keep away from youthful passions, and pursue righteousness, faithfulness, love, and peace, in company with others who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
NET.23: But reject foolish and ignorant controversies, because you know they breed infighting.
NET.24: And the Lord’s slave must not engage in heated disputes but be kind toward all, an apt teacher, patient,
NET.25: correcting opponents with gentleness. Perhaps God will grant them repentance and then knowledge of the truth
NET.26: and they will come to their senses and escape the devil’s trap where they are held captive to do his will.
